fields.summary: SonarQube quality gate always failing on generated code we cannot modify
fields.description: Our project uses protobuf-generated Java classes and the SonarQube quality gate keeps failing because of issues in these generated files. We have no control over the generated code. The quality gate failure is blocking our entire release pipeline. We have tried adding exclusions but nothing seems to work consistently. This is the third time we are raising this issue.

content.text: We tried sonar.exclusions=**/generated/** but the files are under src/main/java not a generated directory so the pattern does not match. Our build generates the files directly into the main source tree.
Try excluding by file name pattern instead of directory. Use sonar.exclusions=**/*Proto.java,**/*Grpc.java if your generated files follow a naming convention.

The naming pattern exclusion partially works but there are still 12 generated files that do not follow the naming convention. We cannot rename them as they are auto-generated by the protobuf compiler. This is still blocking our release. We have been stuck on this for two weeks now.

You can use the @SuppressWarnings annotation on generated classes or configure your protobuf plugin to output to a separate source directory that you can then exclude. Closing this ticket as the workaround has been provided.

This is still not properly resolved. The annotation approach does not work for all rule types and changing the protobuf output directory would require changes to 6 other build scripts. We are just bypassing the quality gate manually for now which defeats the purpose entirely. This will come back again.
